posts.title: Best resources for DSA?
posts.content: I’m currently preparing for placements and feeling overwhelmed because there are so many DSA resources available online. Some people recommend Striver’s sheet, others say LeetCode is enough, while some suggest Codeforces for problem solving. I’m in my second year and want to build strong fundamentals before internship season starts. Can anyone suggest a proper roadmap that worked for them?

posts.title: Tailwind vs CSS debate
posts.content: I’ve been building projects using plain CSS for a long time, but recently everyone seems to be moving toward Tailwind CSS. Some developers say it improves speed and maintainability, while others say it makes JSX messy and unreadable. I’m confused whether I should invest time learning Tailwind deeply or continue mastering traditional CSS and SCSS.

posts.title: Python vs Java for DSA
posts.content: I’ve noticed most people prefer Python for DSA because of its simpler syntax and faster coding during interviews. However, I already know Java pretty well and feel comfortable with object-oriented programming concepts. I’m wondering if switching languages now would waste time or if Python would actually give me an advantage during coding rounds.

posts.title: AI replacing developers?
posts.content: With AI tools improving rapidly, many students around me are worried that frontend, backend, and even software engineering jobs may disappear in the future. At the same time, companies are still hiring developers who understand system design, architecture, and product thinking. Do you think AI will replace developers completely, or will it simply change how developers work?

posts.title: How to stay consistent in coding?
posts.content: I start learning consistently for a few days and feel very motivated, but after a week I lose momentum and start procrastinating. Sometimes I compare myself with people on LinkedIn who seem far ahead in their careers. How do you stay disciplined in coding?

posts.title: How I got my first internship
posts.content: Last year I had zero confidence in coding. After months of DSA practice, project building, and interview prep, I finally landed my first internship after multiple rejections. Sharing this because many students feel stuck right now.
